qualifications.transferable_skills: 19 years of progressive management experience in financial services directly translates to operating a Kumon franchise. Skills include: financial planning and P&L management (directly applicable to center revenue and expense management), staff recruitment and performance management (applicable to hiring and developing Kumon instructors), client relationship management (applicable to parent and student enrollment retention), regulatory compliance and documentation (applicable to Kumon operational standards and franchise compliance requirements), and strategic planning (applicable to enrollment growth targets and market penetration in Cedar Park).

business_plan.business_description: Chen Learning Centers LLC operates a Kumon Math and Reading Center franchise in Cedar Park, Texas. Kumon is the world's largest after-school supplemental education program, with over 4 million students enrolled globally across 60 countries. The Cedar Park center will provide individualized math and reading instruction to students from pre-K through Grade 12, using the proven Kumon self-learning methodology developed by Toru Kumon in 1954.
business_plan.market_opportunity: Cedar Park is one of the fastest-growing communities in the Austin metropolitan area with a population of approximately 85,000 residents. The city has a high concentration of families with school-age children, driven by significant technology sector employment from companies including Dell, Apple, and numerous Austin-based technology firms. The school district serves over 20,000 students. Demand for supplemental education is strong — the nearest competing Kumon center is 8.4 miles away in Round Rock.
business_plan.revenue_model: Monthly tuition fees per subject per student. Current Kumon rate: $160 per subject per month. Average enrolled student purchases 1.4 subjects. Target enrollment by end of Year 1: 75 students. Target enrollment by end of Year 2: 120 students. Target enrollment by end of Year 3: 160 students (center capacity).

year_1.notes: Ramp-up year. Enrollment builds from 0 to 75 students over 12 months.

year_2.notes: Growth year. Word-of-mouth referrals drive enrollment acceleration.

year_3.notes: Maturity. Center reaches target capacity. Royalty obligations fully offset by volume.

business_plan.non_marginality_argument: The Kumon Cedar Park center will not be marginal. At target enrollment of 75 students in Year 1, monthly gross revenue of $16,800 comfortably exceeds monthly operating costs of $12,200 including rent, royalties, staffing, and overhead. The business will generate net income of approximately $2,200 per month by Month 8 of operations and $26,400 in Year 1 net income. By Year 3, the center projects $143,600 in net income, well above the threshold required to support the investor and his family. The Kumon franchise model has a 70-year proven track record. The Cedar Park location was selected based on demographic analysis showing strong demand and limited competition within an 8-mile radius.
business_plan.develop_and_direct: Michael Chen will direct and develop the enterprise on a day-to-day basis. He will not engage in passive investment. His activities will include: hiring and training all center staff, overseeing daily center operations during the ramp-up phase, managing student enrollment and parent communications, implementing Kumon's operational standards and curriculum delivery requirements, managing the center's financial performance against projections, and executing local marketing and community outreach programs. He will be present at the center a minimum of 40 hours per week.

source_of_funds_narrative.summary: The $185,000 investment in Chen Learning Centers LLC was funded entirely from Michael Chen's personal assets — no loans, no borrowed funds, no third-party investors. The funds originated from two sources: personal savings accumulated over 14 years of employment in financial services ($110,000), and net proceeds from the sale of a recreational property in Muskoka, Ontario ($75,000).
source_of_funds_narrative.savings_detail: From 2001 through 2025, Michael Chen maintained a personal savings program, directing a portion of his employment income into a combination of TFSA accounts, RRSP accounts, and non-registered investment accounts held at RBC. The $110,000 savings component represents the liquidation of non-registered investment holdings in June 2025.
source_of_funds_narrative.property_sale_detail: In August 2025, Michael Chen sold a recreational property located at 4421 Bala Falls Road, Bala, Ontario, for $295,000. The property was purchased in 2015 for $165,000. After paying the outstanding mortgage balance of $48,000, agent commission of $14,750, and legal fees of $2,800, the net proceeds were $229,450. Of this amount, $75,000 was designated for the U.S. business investment. The remainder was retained in Canada.

non_immigrant_intent.intent_statement: Michael and Jennifer Chen intend to return to Canada upon the expiration or termination of E-2 status. Their intent is to operate the Kumon franchise for the initial E-2 period, develop it to full enrollment capacity, and then evaluate options including franchise resale or appointment of a Canadian manager to allow remote oversight. Their long-term retirement plans are in Canada where their extended family, pensions, and retirement savings remain. The E-2 visa is a temporary business investment opportunity, not an immigration pathway.

writing_style_sample: I have spent nearly two decades helping people make the most important financial decisions of their lives. At RBC, my job has always been to cut through complexity and give people a clear picture of where they stand and what their options are. That same instinct is what drew me to the Kumon franchise model. The methodology works. The numbers work. And Cedar Park is exactly the kind of growing community where families are actively looking for what Kumon offers. I am not coming to the United States to take a chance. I am coming to execute a plan I have been building toward for three years.
